titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Larry Fagin Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8888432.
Boxes: B, D, E, F, J-53, 55-59

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 24, 2021 at 03:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 55 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 56-58 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Access to original audiovisual recordings (Reel 714a) is restricted. Consult the Beinecke Library Access Services department for information about reference copies.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2008 and 2018.
January 2021 acquisition: Purchased from Granary Books on the George B. Alvord Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into three groupings: November 2008 acquisition, 1956-2008; May 2018 acquisition, 1918-2017; January 2021 acquisition, circa 1970-2016.

bioghist.head: Larry Fagin
bioghist.p: Larry Fagin (1937-2017) was an American poet and educator. Fagin graduated from the University of Maryland. He was the author or co-author of numerous collections of poetry, including Parade of the Caterpillars (1968), Brain Damage (1971), Two Serious Poems and One Other , with Bill Berkson (1971), Landscape , with George Schneeman (1972), The World of Leon , with Ron Padgett (1973), Rhymes of a Jerk (1974), Stabs (1979), Lime Rickeys , with Jack Collom (1994), and Dig & Delve , with Trevor Winkfield (1999). His poetry and prose also appeared in magazines, such as Paris Review , Blue Suede Shoes , Poetry , Open Space , and Rolling Stone , as well as several anthologies of poetry.

Fagin published and edited Adventures in Poetry magazine (1968-1975) and Un Poco Loco , a monthly poetry newsletter (1978-1979). He was co-director of the Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church 1971 to 1976, and a founder and director of Danspace from 1975 to 1980. Starting in 1968, Fagin taught creative writing to public school students and teachers. He was on the faculty at the Naropa Institute from 1977 to 1987, and chaired the Poetics Department from 1982 to 1984. He also taught at UCLA and New School University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Larry Fagin Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and organization. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of material created and accumulated by Larry Fagin, stemming from his individual and collaborative work as a poet and editor, and, as well as his work as a teacher, filmmaker, artist, choreographer, and journalist. It also documents his personal and working relationships with other poets and writers associated with the New York School. Material includes correspondence, printed material, typescripts of poems, essays, translations, and film scripts, publicity material for readings, lectures, and publications, interview transcripts, teaching files, collages, an 8 mm motion picture, and other material. The bulk of the collection consists of files compiled by Fagin according to correspondent and/or subject, including extensive files for Glen Baxter, Bill Berkson, Jack Collom, Clark Coolidge, Michael Friedman, John Godfrey, Naropa Institute, Ron Padgett, Poetry Project at St. Mark's, Judith Reifman, and Anne Waldman, among others.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Larry Fagin Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: St. Mark's Church In-the-Bowery (New York, N.Y.). Poetry Project

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, printed material, typescripts of poems, essays, translations, and film scripts, publicity material for readings, lectures, and publications, interview transcripts, teaching files, artwork, audiovisual materials, and other papers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by surname of individual.

did.unittitle: Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into five subseries: Poems, Collaborative Works, Translations, Essays, and Related Files.

did.unittitle: "Notes from the Cracks in the Floor"
Berkson, Bill; Fagin, Joan Inglis; Mitchell, Tessie; Padgett, Ron; Warsh, Lewis - "Leon Poems"
Berkson, Bill; Borregaard, Ebbe; Kyger, Joanne - "Song of Offering"
Berkson, Bill; Carroll, Jim; Veitch, Tom - "Things We'd Like to Do to Anne Waldman (on her birthday April 2, 1970)"
Berrigan, Ted - "Plain Speaking Brilliant Manifesto for the Record"
"The Atmosphere of the Other Guy", cover illustrations, photocopies
Gallup, Dick; Hollo, Anselm; Price, Laurie - "Tho you are going...wait a minute"
Padgett, Ron; Schneeman, George - "The Poems of George Schneeman"
Tuttle, Richard - "Poems by Larry Fagin, Drawings by Richard Tuttle"
Cocteau, Jean - "La Fin du Potomak" (1939) and "Testament of Orpheus"
Mayakovsky, Vladimir - "An Extraordinary Adventure Which Happened to Me, Vladimir Mayakovsky, in a Summer Cottage"
Scheerbarth, Paul - from "Katerpoesie" and prose (co-translated with Evelyn Thureau)
Draft of memoir for Angel Hair Sleeps with a Boy in My Head
"New American Poetry 1950-1970" for Encyclopedia of Poetry
"Polka Dots and Mean Booms" for Shuffle Boil Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press releases, reviews, contracts, royalty statements relating to the publication of certain works; transcripts, clippings, and email printouts of interviews of Fagin; and various lyrics for songs and sheet music.

did.unittitle: Various lyrics for songs and sheet music

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into seven subseries: Editorial Files, Teaching Files, Film Files, Art Files, Choreography Files, Journalism Files, and Correspondence and Other Papers.

did.unittitle: Publishers Weekly review of "The Green Lake is Awake: by Joseph Ceravolo
Notes for proposed "Selected Poems of Clark Coolidge"
Notes for unpublished anthology of American poetry titled "Big"
"Portraits and Home Movies," Super 8mm motion picture

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to original audiovisual recordings is restricted. Consult the Beinecke Library Access Services department for information about reference copies.

did.unittitle: Collage, "I have forbidden women to sleep with cream"
Flyer design by Larry Fagin for Warren Sonbert's film showing, "Carriage Trade"
Press packet for Rolling Stone Salvador Dali event
Article on Laurent de Brunhoff for US magazine
Curriculum vita and material regarding grants
Material relating to New York Public Library Exhibition, " A Secret Location on the Lower East Side: Adventures in Writing, 1960-1980"
Material relating to poetry readings, lectures, and publications
Item list of collection contents created by Fagin
"Portraits and Home Movies," Super 8mm motion picture (Reel 714a)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to original audiovisual recordings (Reel 714a) is restricted. Consult the Beinecke Library Access Services department for information about reference copies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition, with some oversized materials placed in appropriate housing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, drafts and typescripts of poems, essays, and other writings, ephemera, photographs, teaching files, artwork, audiovisual materials and electronic, and other materials that document Fagin's life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 55 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 56-58 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence between Larry Fagin and other poets, artists, and individuals, as well as subject files containing clippings, notes, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Glen Baxter--Around the World with Tilley Kettle
Glen Baxter--Drawings for "Drawings" Adventures in Poetry 1974
Glen Baxter--The Handy Guide to Amazing People/12 original drawings/Gotham Book Mart
Glen Baxter--"The Khaki"/3 drawings for Adventures in Poetry
Glen Baxter--The Real Book of True Facts
Glen Baxter--St. Marks Church April 17 original drawing and printed flyer
North, Charles--the nearness of the way you look tonight
Photocopies of Philip Whalen Project materials

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive writings by Fagin, including drafts and versions of works created in collaboration with other poets and artists, editorial projects, and journalistic articles. Also includes project files including notes, clippings, drafts, documentation, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: LF- Assorted Poems (some collabs-Jack Collum, Peter Orlovsky...)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains poems and correspondence between Fagin and accepted poets for the publication he was editing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains printed material on poems throughout history by British poets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a cover for the Sal Mimeo publication "Numero Zero."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains copies of potential cover pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains poems, correspondence, cover pages, and photocopies related to Sal Mimeo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript of Sal Mimeo 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript of Sal Mimeo #5.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains edits of Sal Mimeo #5.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript of Sal Mimeo 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains compiled poems and photocopies related to Sal Mimeo 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript for Sal Mimeo 7 (no cover page) and a contact list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a copy of the Sal Mimeo 7 master manuscript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript of Sal Mimeo 8.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains cover pages and 3 poems from Sal Mimeo 8.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a cover page and table of contents for Sal Mimeo 9 and 10, and a photo of Fagin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains collections of poems and cover pages for Sal Mimeo, "Moving Blanket" by Kostos Anagnopoulos; "The Way to Oblong," by Karin Randolph; and a series of other poems and novellas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains manuscripts from the collection of poems Neglecterinos, likely edited by Fagin, and Sal Mimeo Zero.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains correspondence, a postcard, wedding invitation, disability claims, social security and medical information, writings, student loan information, essays, transcripts, college applications, and recommendations, among other things.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers documenting Larry Fagin's personal life and business dealings including documents related to Fagin family history, Fagin's work as a teacher, financial records, audiovisual materials, and electronic media.

did.unittitle: Fagin and Ted Bernan--Plain Speaking Brilliant Manifesto
Fagin and Coolidge--The Atmosphere of the Other Guy
Fagin and Coolidge--Great White Coast Jazz Suicides
Ron Padgett and Larry Fagin conversation
Fagin and Ron Padgett--World of Wheels
Fagin and Ron Padgett--Food of the Gods
Fagin and Ethie Stearns--News from Nowhere
Fagin and Anne Waldman--on Frank O'Hara
Fagin and Trevor Winkfield--on Dig and Delve
A Barnum and Bailey World--Beginning of an anthology
Fagin and Porter Fox--Short story anthology
A Secret Location on the Lower East Side
Grant proposal for Mid-Atlantic Arts Foundation
O'Hara Talk for the New School
Allen Ginsberg and the New York School--notes for a talk
Sophisticated American Primitives, Primitive American Sophisticates--USC Talk on Feb. 19, 1998
Notes from "The Piazza Poems of Harlan Dangerfield"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains writings and correspondence by Fagin's wife, Susan Noels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains information and forms regarding social security.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains veterinary information and photos of SKN's cat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains medication, invoices, and lab results for Fagin and Noel's medical procedures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains social security information, court forms, taxes information, medical information, and a birthday card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a photocopy of a brief autobiographical sketch of Fagin's early life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains letters and a Boy Scout cub card from Fagin's childhood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains copies and originals of Fagin's degrees and transcripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains Fagin's original birth certificate and a renewed copy, both from New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains the crematory certificate for Fagin's death and also the memorial certificate of Lucy (cat).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains memorial pamphlets, letters, and a wedding announcement concerning Fagin's parents.

did.unittitle: Ted Greenwald--Moving Parts and Comma Fork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains souvenirs, receipts, and maps from a trip to France and Austria.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection includes lists of poems, contacts, classes, music, writers, words, and collected business cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection includes notes, research, and articles on music.

did.unittitle: Cassette tape liners for WS Burroughs and T Veitch recordings

did.unittitle: The Climate of New York--Rudy Burckhardt 1947

Angel Hair Reading vol. 2, 3/8/02, New College, SF

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition, with some oversized materials placed in appropriate housing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Fagin and other individuals, events, and locations, along with artwork and a number of notebooks containing writings and sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains photographs of Fagin, friends, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains mostly 9x11 inch photographs of Fagin, friends, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains photographs by John Sarsgard, related to 47 poets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains photographs possibly taken by John Sarsgard, of poets and SKN (Fagin's wife).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This set of photographs contains mostly family and travel photogrpaphs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains photographs of family and friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains photos of family and travel likely dating back to 19th century, with main bulk from mid 20th century.

did.unittitle: Personal, art, and pop culture photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains predominately portraits of Fagin by Wolfgang Hageney as well as photographs and film of peers and art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains one portrait grouped with a series of pen drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains 2 paintings by George Schneeman in collaboration with Fagin.
